name: UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
description: Stabilise GHG concentrations; no binding numeric target. Ireland signed 1992, ratified 1994.

name: Kyoto Protocol — 1st commitment period
description: Ireland permitted +13% above 1990 over 2008–2012 (within EU burden-sharing). In force 2005.

name: National Climate Change Strategy (1st)
description: First national strategy; sectoral measures toward Kyoto. Reviewed 2002.
outcome: Largely missed; emissions rose ~31% above 1990 by 2001

name: EU Emissions Trading System (ETS) Directive 2003/87/EC
description: Cap-and-trade on large installations; operational from 2005.
outcome: Operational; emissions in ETS sector declining

name: Ireland's Pathway to Kyoto Compliance
description: Review setting out route to Kyoto compliance; basis for 2007 strategy.

name: National Climate Change Strategy 2007–2012
agency: Dept of Environment, Heritage & Local Govt
description: Sectoral measures to meet Kyoto target; first adaptation section.
outcome: Emissions fell mainly via 2008 recession, not policy

name: Carbon Fund Act 2007
description: Established Carbon Fund to buy Kyoto units to meet international obligations.

name: 2020 Climate & Energy Package — Effort Sharing Decision 406/2009
description: Ireland: −20% non-ETS emissions by 2020 vs 2005, with annual limits.
outcome: Missed via domestic action; ~5.7% delivered, used flexibilities

name: Renewable Energy Directive (RED I) 2009/28/EC
description: Ireland: 16% of gross final energy from renewables by 2020.

name: Carbon Tax (Finance Act 2010)
description: Tax on CO2 of non-ETS fuels; legislated trajectory to €100/tonne by 2030; revenue ring-fenced.

name: Food Harvest 2020
description: Expansion of dairy & beef output and exports.

name: National Climate Change Adaptation Framework
description: First national adaptation framework.

name: EU milk quota abolition (CAP)
description: End of milk quotas effective 2015; enabled dairy expansion.

name: Paris Agreement
description: Limit warming to well below 2°C, pursue 1.5°C; NDCs. Adopted 2015; EU & Ireland ratified 2016.

name: Climate Action & Low Carbon Development Act 2015
description: First Irish climate law; created Climate Change Advisory Council; National Policy Position (−80% CO2 by 2050 in energy/built/transport; carbon-neutral agriculture & land use).
outcome: Framework in place; criticised as non-binding on targets

name: Food Wise 2025
description: Continued agri-food export expansion to 2025.
outcome: Associated with further emissions & water-quality pressure

name: National Mitigation Plan
agency: Dept of Communications, Climate Action & Environment
description: First statutory mitigation plan under the 2015 Act.
outcome: Quashed by Supreme Court (2020, 'Climate Case Ireland') as too vague

name: Effort Sharing Regulation (EU) 2018/842
description: Ireland: −30% non-ETS by 2030 vs 2005 (later revised to −42%).

name: Renewable Energy Directive II (RED II) 2018/2001
description: EU 32% renewables by 2030; sectoral sub-targets (transport, heat).
outcome: Transposed; national RES target later raised

name: Governance of the Energy Union Regulation (EU) 2018/1999
description: Requires Member States to submit National Energy & Climate Plans (NECPs).

name: Project Ireland 2040 / National Development Plan
description: Long-term capital plan with climate-action allocations.

name: European Green Deal
description: Roadmap to EU climate neutrality by 2050; spawned Fit for 55, REPowerEU, Green Deal Industrial Plan.

name: Climate Action Plan 2019
description: First detailed CAP; over 180 actions; non-statutory.
outcome: Partially implemented; superseded by statutory CAPs

name: National Energy & Climate Plan 2021–2030 (NECP)
description: First NECP; committed to ~7% average annual emissions cut 2021–2030.

name: Climate Case Ireland (Supreme Court ruling 2020)
description: Friends of the Irish Environment v Government; quashed 2017 National Mitigation Plan (decided July 2020).

name: Programme for Government 2020
description: Committed to 7% average annual emissions reduction and stronger climate law.

name: Ag Climatise — agriculture roadmap
description: Roadmap toward climate neutrality for agriculture; a living document.

name: European Climate Law (EU) 2021/1119
description: Legally binds EU to net-zero by 2050 and net −55% by 2030 vs 1990.

name: Fit for 55 package (adopted 2023)
description: 13+ revised laws to hit −55% by 2030: revised ESR (Ireland −42% vs 2005), LULUCF, ETS reform, ETS2 (2027), CBAM (from 2026), RED III.

name: Climate Action & Low Carbon Development (Amendment) Act 2021
description: National climate objective: climate-neutral economy by 2050; −51% by 2030 vs 2018; 5-yr carbon budgets; sectoral emissions ceilings. Commenced Sep 2021.
outcome: Cornerstone law; binding governance in place

name: Climate Action Plan 2021 (Securing Our Future)
description: First CAP under the 2021 Act; sectoral pathways to −51% by 2030.

name: Food Vision 2030
description: 10-yr agri-food strategy; aims for a climate-neutral food system.

name: Carbon Budgets & Sectoral Emissions Ceilings
description: Carbon budgets effective Apr 2022 (2021–25; 2026–30; 2031–35); sectoral ceilings adopted Jul 2022 (e.g. agriculture −25% by 2030).

name: Climate Action Plan 2023
description: First statutory annual CAP; aligns actions with carbon budgets & ceilings.

name: Climate Action Plan 2024 (published Dec 2023)
description: Refines measures to deliver budgets & ceilings.

name: Updated NECP 2021–2030 (Jul 2024)
description: Renewables share raised to 43% by 2030; renewable electricity (RES-E) 80% by 2030.

name: Infrastructure, Climate & Nature Fund (Budget 2024)
description: Long-term fund (€4bn by end-2025) for emissions, water-quality & biodiversity projects.

name: Climate Action Plan 2025 (published Apr 2025)
description: Third statutory annual update; read with CAP24; detailed implementation focus.

name: 51% Emission Reduction Target
description: Statutory national target under the Climate Act 2021: −51% greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 relative to a 2018 baseline.

name: Climate-Neutral Ireland
description: National climate objective under the Climate Act 2021: a climate-neutral economy no later than 2050.
